next_steps: Urgent CT angiography of the aorta (with contrast) to evaluate for aneurysm expansion or rupture
Transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to evaluate for valvular vegetations and aortic root involvement
Continuous cardiac monitoring and stabilization of blood pressure
Consultation with vascular surgery and cardiothoracic surgery for potential emergent intervention
Continued targeted intravenous antibiotic therapy based on MSSA sensitivity
Repeat blood cultures to monitor for persistent bacteremia

differential_diagnoses: Mediastinitis or mediastinal abscess secondary to aortic infection
next_steps: Immediate CT angiography of the chest/abdomen and pelvis to evaluate for interval change in aortic diameter and periaortic inflammation
Urgent transesophageal echocardiography (TEE) to evaluate for valvular vegetations and aortic root involvement
Immediate consultation with cardiothoracic and vascular surgery for emergent intervention planning
Continuation of high-dose intravenous anti-staphylococcal therapy (e.g., nafcillin or cefazolin) and infectious disease consultation
Repeat blood cultures daily until clearance of bacteremia is documented
Continuous hemodynamic monitoring and blood pressure management to reduce aortic wall stress
MRI of the thoracic spine and sternoclavicular joints to rule out occult osteomyelitis
